CREATE TABLE time_testing (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIME(6));
SELECT * FROM time_testing WHERE event_time >= '12:00:00.000000' AND event_time <= '18:00:00.000000';
SELECT id FROM time_testing WHERE event_time = (SELECT MAX(event_time) FROM time_testing);
SELECT event_time FROM time_testing WHERE event_time BETWEEN '14:00:00.000000' AND '16:00:00.000000' ORDER BY event_time DESC;
SELECT t1.id, t1.event_time FROM time_testing AS t1 JOIN time_testing AS t2 ON t1.id != t2.id WHERE t1.event_time < t2.event_time;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM time_testing WHERE event_time < '01:00:00.000000';
SELECT event_time, CASE WHEN event_time < '12:00:00.000000' THEN 'Morning' ELSE 'Afternoon' END AS time_period FROM time_testing;
UPDATE time_testing SET event_time = '20:00:00.000000' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, event_time FROM time_testing WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL ORDER BY event_time;
SELECT event_time FROM time_testing WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM time_testing WHERE event_time LIKE '12:%');
CREATE TABLE event_log (event_id INT, log_time TIME(3), FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES time_testing(id));
SELECT t.event_time, e.log_time FROM time_testing t JOIN event_log e ON t.id = e.event_id WHERE e.log_time < '12:30:00.000';
